name: Airtable
description: Airtable is a cloud spreadsheet-database hybrid for building collaborative apps and workflows with a REST API, SDKs, webhooks, and OAuth integration.

auth_methods.note: Official docs state Airtable supports OAuth 2.0 and Personal access tokens (used as Bearer tokens). The docs also reference legacy API key usage; present auth methods are OAuth and personal access tokens (Bearer). 'other' included to reflect legacy API key mention in docs.

credential_access.note: The support article documents how a user creates personal access tokens from their account settings; the public pricing page shows a Free plan, and the getting-started guide links to token creation—indicating developers can obtain tokens via self-serve without human approval.

api_surface.note: The API reference at https://airtable.com/api is an auto-generated REST API for bases (endpoints for tables, records, attachments, fields). The Developers hub also lists SDKs and webhooks, but the primary documented surface is a comprehensive REST API.

buildability.note: Public REST docs and self-service personal access tokens (or OAuth app registration) are available, so an integration/agent can be developed today.

queries: Airtable REST API docs base endpoints
How to get Airtable API key personal access token
